1. to solve the equation $ P(x) = 50 $, we set $ -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 = 50 $ and solve for $ x $. this involves rearranging the equation to $ -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 70 = 0 $. the students will use numerical methods or graphing to find the value of $ x $ where the performance score equals 50.  

2. to determine the range of years $ x $ where $ P(x) > 60 $, the students will analyze the polynomial $ P(x) = -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 $ and find the values of $ x $ where the performance score exceeds 60. this will involve evaluating the polynomial at various points and identifying the interval where $ P(x) > 60 $.
